rows.title: Spain's AEPD Launches Interactive Tool for Public Consultation of Personal Data Breach Notifications
rows.summary: AEPD published a new interactive consultation tool providing filterable, visual access to personal data breach notifications received under Article 33 GDPR. The Agency states the tool complements existing monthly PDF statistical reports and forms part of its 2025–2030 Strategic Plan on open government and digital transformation.
rows.body_name: Agencia Espanola de Proteccion de Datos

rows.title: Spain CNMV Issues Circular CIR/DTSA/010/25 on Securities and Financial Markets Supervision
rows.summary:
The Comisión Nacional del Mercado de Valores (CNMV), Spain's securities market regulator, has issued Circular CIR/DTSA/010/25, a supervisory or technical circular addressed to regulated entities within its jurisdiction covering securities, trading, or financial market conduct matters.

On the date of issuance recorded under reference CIR/DTSA/010/25, the CNMV published the circular through its Dirección de Técnica y Supervisión de Actividades (DTSA), the internal directorate responsible for technical supervision of market activities and participants. The alphanumeric reference follows the CNMV's standard internal classification convention, in which 'CIR' denotes a circular, 'DTSA' identifies the issuing directorate, '010' indicates it is the tenth circular in the relevant sequence, and '25' refers to the year 2025.

CNMV circulars issued under the DTSA directorate typically address supervisory requirements, reporting obligations, technical standards, or interpretive guidance applicable to investment firms, collective investment scheme managers, trading venues, or other supervised entities operating under Spanish securities law, including the Ley del Mercado de Valores y de los Servicios de Inversión (Ley 6/2023, de 17 de marzo). Such circulars carry binding or quasi-binding regulatory weight for addressees within the CNMV's supervisory perimeter, complementing Royal Decrees and Ministerial Orders that form the primary legislative framework.

The specific substantive content, scope of application, affected entity categories, and any associated deadlines or transitional provisions of CIR/DTSA/010/25 were not available from the source material reviewed. The official text, together with any accompanying explanatory notes or consultation records, would be accessible through the CNMV's official circular register and its dedicated regulatory documentation portal at cnmv.es. Entities subject to CNMV supervision are required to consult the official published text to determine their obligations under this instrument.

rows.title: Spain's INCIBE and AESIA Launch Joint Awareness Campaign on AI Use and Digital Safety for Young People
rows.summary: INCIBE and AESIA jointly launched an awareness campaign on AI use + digital safety for young people. 3 audiences (families, teachers, young people themselves), 3 thematic blocks released over coming weeks. Materials: blog articles, infographics, podcasts, online workshop. Topics include identifying AI-generated content, classroom AI risks/opportunities, parent guidance.

rows.title: Spain's Four Data Protection Authorities Sign Joint Declaration on RGPD Tenth Anniversary
rows.summary: On the tenth anniversary of the GDPR, Spain's four data protection authorities — AEPD, APDCAT, AVPD, and CTPDA — jointly signed an institutional declaration committing to deeper cooperation, common enforcement approaches, support for DPOs, and a strengthened privacy culture in the face of digital transformation, the data economy, and AI.
rows.body_name: Agencia Espanola de Proteccion de Datos

rows.title: Spain's AEPD Launches Public Network of Research Groups on Privacy and Emerging Technologies
rows.summary: AEPD launched a public network integrating ~100 research groups, initiatives, and projects on privacy, data protection, and emerging technologies including AI. Partnership with the ValgrAI Foundation (Generalitat Valenciana + 5 Valencian public universities + business partners) handles dissemination + coordination. Forms part of the AEPD Laboratory programme.
rows.body_name: Agencia Espanola de Proteccion de Datos

rows.title: AEPD Sets Out Personal Data Breach Notification Requirements Under Art. 33 GDPR
rows.summary:
The Agencia Española de Protección de Datos has published guidance clarifying the obligations of data controllers to notify personal data breaches to the supervisory authority under Article 33 of the GDPR, including the applicable 72-hour deadline and the conditions determining AEPD's territorial competence.

On 24 March 2026, the Agencia Española de Protección de Datos published updated guidance on its website setting out the procedural and substantive requirements for breach notification to the supervisory authority. No fine was associated with this publication.

The AEPD states that a personal data breach constitutes a security incident resulting in the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorised disclosure of, or access to, personal data processed by a controller. The AEPD notes that such incidents may cause physical, material or immaterial harm to individuals. The AEPD states that Article 33 GDPR obliges controllers to notify the competent supervisory authority when a breach is likely to pose a risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons, and that where the risk is high, controllers are additionally required to communicate the breach to affected individuals in accordance with Article 34 GDPR. The AEPD further states that the notification deadline is 72 hours from the moment the organisation becomes aware of the breach.

The AEPD states that the conditions under which it is the competent authority for private-sector controllers are: where the controller's sole establishment is in Spain; where the controller has multiple EU establishments and its main establishment is in Spain; or, where the controller has no EU establishment, where affected individuals are located in Spain. For public-sector entities, the AEPD states that public administrations are generally required to notify it of breaches, with exceptions applying to certain autonomous communities in relation to public-sector entities under their own competence.

The AEPD states that notifications are required to be submitted electronically via the breach notification form on the Sede Electrónica, in order to meet the requirements of Article 33.3 GDPR. The AEPD further states that where a controller determines no risk to individuals exists, the controller nonetheless remains obligated to document the breach, its effects and any corrective measures taken, enabling the AEPD to verify compliance with Article 33 GDPR. The AEPD notes that timely notification constitutes evidence of a controller's diligence, while failure to notify where the obligation applies is classified as an infringement under the GDPR.
rows.body_name: Agencia Espanola de Proteccion de Datos

rows.title: Spain's Four Data Protection Authorities Publish Joint Decalogue on Data Protection in Educational Platforms
rows.summary: AEPD + APDCAT + AVPD + CTPDA jointly published a decalogue establishing 10 basic principles for personal data protection in digital educational platforms. Targets: education administrations, schools, EdTech vendors. Justification: large volumes of minors' personal data + non-optional use as part of educational activity. Preventive approach across all autonomous communities.
rows.body_name: Agencia Espanola de Proteccion de Datos

rows.title: Spain's INCIBE and CONETIC Publish 2025 Cybersecurity Industry Study Showing Sector Growth
rows.summary: INCIBE + CONETIC (Spanish Confederation of ICT Companies) published the «Estudio sobre la industria de la ciberseguridad en España 2025». Findings: cybersecurity is among the most dynamic sectors of Spain's digital economy with growth in employment, revenue, number of firms, and projected growth. Persistent female-participation gap flagged by INCIBE Director General Félix Barrio.

rows.title: Spain's INCIBE Detected 122,000 Cybersecurity Incidents in 2025 and Handled 142,767 Helpline Queries (+45% YoY)
rows.summary: INCIBE-CERT managed 122,000+ cyber incidents in 2025 and proactively detected/notified 237,028 vulnerable systems exposed on internet. NIS2-essential operators: 401 incidents handled. «Tu Ayuda en Ciberseguridad» 017 helpline: 142,767 queries (+44.9% YoY); 49% preventive.

rows.title: AEPD Publishes PDPC Anonymisation Tool for Excel-Based Data Processing
rows.summary:
The Agencia Española de Protección de Datos (AEPD) has published a downloadable anonymisation tool developed by the Personal Data Protection Commission (PDPC), made available via the AEPD website to assist organisations in anonymising personal data in compliance with applicable data protection requirements.

On 9 April 2025, the AEPD published the PDPC Anonymisation Tool on its website, providing a freely downloadable Excel-based utility intended to support data anonymisation processes under the applicable data protection framework, including Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR).

The AEPD states that the tool is distributed as an encrypted archive to prevent antivirus software from quarantining the Excel spreadsheet file. The AEPD states that the archive is extractable using 7-Zip or equivalent software with the password "DIT-AEPD". The AEPD further notes that Microsoft Excel may prevent the execution of macros within the file, and that users are required to unblock the file manually by accessing the file properties and selecting the "unblock" option.

The AEPD states that the anonymisation tool was developed and tested using Excel versions 2010, 2016, and 2019 on the Microsoft Windows operating system, and the authority notes that the tool may not function correctly on other Excel versions within Windows. The AEPD further states that users are required to configure the regional format setting on their device to "Español (España)" via the Windows Region settings in order for the tool to operate as intended. The AEPD provides a SHA-256 hash value of 30ab954cc35f67bc2666a8ac6d94676fceb314e9552863592c0895bccf846695 for users to verify the integrity and authenticity of the downloaded file by running the certutil command via the Windows Command Prompt against the downloaded zip file named "herramienta-anonimizacion-pdpc-es.zip".
rows.body_name: Agencia Espanola de Proteccion de Datos
